subject: Modify ansible version in docker/chroot
commit_message:
Modify ansible version in docker/chroot

Updating to ansible version: 2.7.8
Current ansible version: 2.6.3

The current version breaks json values in playbooks.

Change-Id: I9858339a08711ad921122b2e7b6ccf04d62454a3
Issue-ID: OOM-1698
Signed-off-by: Petr Ospalý <p.ospaly@partner.samsung.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

good excuse to update ansible. I think we have also other better reasons to update it anyway.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

In previous patchset I used 2.7.1 because that was the version in my debian stable, but we can raise it to the latest, their will be bugs anyway - all minor versions have bugfixes:
https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/stable-2.7/changelogs/CHANGELOG-v2.7.rst
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

tested, works on our playbooks
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

Patch Set 2:

Hold on hold on. Are you going to upgrade ansible just like that without any regression tests? I think at least one full successful deployment should be confirmed with 2.7.8 before we actually upgrade to see if any major changes aren't needed in the code. Have you at least got acquainted with https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/porting_guides/porting_guide_2.7.html ?

Patch Set 2:

Hold on hold on. Are you going to upgrade ansible just like that without any regression tests? I think at least one full successful deployment should be confirmed with 2.7.8 before we actually upgrade to see if any major changes aren't needed in the code. Have you at least got acquainted with https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/porting_guides/porting_guide_2.7.html ?

Patch Set 2:

> Hold on hold on. Are you going to upgrade ansible just like that
 > without any regression tests? I think at least one full successful
 > deployment should be confirmed with 2.7.8 before we actually
 > upgrade to see if any major changes aren't needed in the code. Have
 > you at least got acquainted with https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/porting_guides/porting_guide_2.7.html
 > ?

Yes for sure this cannot just merged very quickly. I hope nobody was planning that. 
And related to that I'm quite suspicious about the verified +1 from Peter as not sure if he really tested whole offline solution. Verified+1 cannot be given based on single ansible module testing is good for this version.

I even saw +2 from Michal and that what made me write this comment as i thought we're merging this right away.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

We lacked that info. +1 then.
Patch Set 3: Verified+1

Patch Set 3:

Milan did test it!

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

in any case this needs to be tested after every merge to master. and everything that goes into master after it must be retested again. ;)
Patch Set 4: Patch Set 3 was rebased
Patch Set 4: Verified+1
Change has been successfully merged by Michal Ptacek
